concepts.site_goal: Run a curated peer-to-peer marketplace for vintage and antique furniture where independent sellers list one-of-a-kind pieces and shoppers browse, save, and purchase with confidence.
audience: collectors of mid-century, art deco, and folk furniture
concepts.description: Wares & Provenance is a clustered, editorial vintage furniture marketplace. The homepage hits with a full-bleed terracotta hero block and a dense salon-wall grid of listings cropped at uneven heights. Browse uses a left-rail faceted filter (era, material, style, region, price, condition, ships-from) over a six-column high-density product grid with hover quicklooks. Product pages are gallery-first with a sticky right-rail buy box, provenance timeline, dimensions diagram, and shipping calculator. Seller storefronts function as mini-shops with banner, bio, policies, and tabbed inventory. A sticky cart drawer slides in from the right across every page, and a Journal section runs long-form stories on dealers, eras, and restoration. Earthen palette of terracotta, ochre, oxblood, moss, and bone with a mixed serif editorial display face (Tiempos-like) paired with a grotesque body (Söhne-like) and a typewriter accent for metadata.
concepts.motif: Salon-style clustered product walls with bold earthen color-blocks, asymmetric editorial captions, hand-stamped lot numbers, and a persistent right-side cart drawer.

pages.layout_pattern: Editorial hero block over a salon-style clustered product wall with category color-blocks
sections: Terracotta hero block with rotating featured-lot headline and 'Shop the Lot' CTA

Inline 'Editor's pick' callout cards interrupting the grid every 12 items

Left nav: Orders, Saved listings, Offers, Followed shops, Addresses, Payment, Settings
Orders table with status pills and condition-on-arrival window countdown

Story grid with mixed-size cards, author byline, and read time

message_intent: Convey that every piece is one-of-a-kind and worth lingering on
Build trust in independent sellers through provenance, ratings, and policies
Make dense inventory feel curated rather than overwhelming
Position the marketplace as both a shop and an editorial authority on vintage
content_model: Listing: title, lot number, price, era, style, materials, dimensions, condition grade, provenance notes, seller, location, shipping options, gallery (6-12 images)
Seller storefront: shop name, banner, bio, location, years active, policies, ratings, inventory tabs (in stock / sold archive / on hold)
Category facets: era (Pre-1900, 1900s, 1920s, 1950s, 1970s, 1990s), style (Mid-Century, Art Deco, Brutalist, Shaker, Bauhaus, Postmodern), material, room, price band, ships-from
Journal article: title, dek, hero image, author, read time, body with pull quotes and shoppable inline product chips
Saved listing: list name, items, price-drop alerts, notes
Order: items, seller, shipping quote, tracking, condition-on-arrival window
interactions: Faceted filter drawer with chip-stack of active filters and result count
Hover quicklook on product cards revealing second image and 'add to saved'
Sticky cart drawer that slides from the right and persists across navigation
Shipping calculator on product page that takes ZIP/postcode and returns freight, white-glove, and pickup quotes
Image gallery with thumbnail rail, pinch-zoom, and a fullscreen lightbox
Make-an-offer modal with seller's accept/decline threshold hint
Save-to-list with multiple named lists and a price-drop bell
asset_needs: Cropped photographs of vintage furniture against warm neutral backdrops
Detail shots showing wood grain, joinery, patina, and maker's marks
Seller portrait photography for storefront headers
Editorial spreads for Journal stories with environmental shots
concepts.mobile_behavior: Filters collapse into a bottom-sheet drawer; product grid drops to two columns; cart drawer becomes a full-screen takeover triggered by a sticky footer bar showing item count and subtotal.

concepts.site_goal: Connect estate-sale pickers and barn-find dealers with collectors hunting honest, unrestored vintage furniture, with radical price transparency and freight logistics baked in.
audience: collectors of unrestored and patinated furniture
renters and first-apartment shoppers hunting cheap character pieces
concepts.description: Barnyard & Bone is a punchier, more flea-market take on the seed: a clustered marketplace built around weekly 'Drops' where dozens of sellers post fresh finds every Thursday at noon. The aesthetic leans into mustard, brick, cream, and ink-black with a chunky display grotesque (Druk-like) slammed against an old-style serif (Caslon-like) for body, plus stenciled lot numbers and price tags rendered as paper labels. Layouts are denser and more chaotic than a typical marketplace: the homepage opens with a countdown to the next Drop and a wall-of-finds masonry grid, browse uses top-bar facet pills plus a column-density slider (3/5/8 across), product pages emphasize honest condition with a 'Patina Report' card and a freight quote module that maps the route. Seller storefronts double as dealer journals; the Journal itself is called 'The Picker's Log' and runs first-person dispatches from estate sales and barn cleanouts.
concepts.motif: Paper price-tags, stenciled lot numbers, masonry walls of finds, and weekly Drop countdowns over a mustard-and-brick palette.

Picker's Log teaser strip with three latest dispatches
How it works three-step explainer (Pickers find / We vet / You bid or buy)
Footer with newsletter, seller application, and freight FAQ

Seller card with picker bio, region, and vehicle range for delivery

pages.layout_pattern: Photo-essay feed titled 'The Picker's Log' with location-tagged dispatches and shoppable hotspots

Region filter chips (Midwest, Northeast, South, Mountain, Coast)

message_intent: Make the marketplace feel like a live, time-bound event rather than a static catalog
Celebrate honest wear and unrestored character over polish
Demystify freight cost and timing so buyers can commit confidently
Give pickers and dealers a stage for their hunt narratives
content_model: Listing: lot number, title, ask price, freight estimate, condition grade with Patina Report (wear, repairs, smells, structural), dimensions, dated photos, seller, pickup region
Drop: scheduled release time, participating sellers, item count, preview thumbnails, countdown
Seller: handle, region, picker bio, vehicle (for delivery range), drop participation, archive
Picker's Log entry: dispatch title, location, date picked, photo essay, items recovered with inline shop links
Saved list and watchlist with Drop-day notifications
Order with freight routing map and ETA
interactions: Drop countdown timer that flips to a live-listing grid at release
Column-density slider toggling 3, 5, or 8 items across
Top-bar facet pills with multi-select and 'Save this search'
Patina Report expandable card with checkboxes for wear, repairs, structural notes, and odor
Freight quote module that geocodes ZIP/postcode and draws a route on a small map
Sticky cart drawer with per-seller pickup-vs-ship toggle
Watchlist alarm icon on each card that sets a Drop-day reminder
Picker's Log photo essays with shoppable image hotspots
asset_needs: On-location photography from estate sales, barns, and warehouses
Studio-lit furniture shots with visible wear, scratches, and original finishes
Paper price-tag and stenciled-number graphic elements
Small route-map illustrations for freight visualization
Picker portraits leaning against trucks or in workshops
concepts.mobile_behavior: Drop countdown pins to the top; facet pills become a horizontal scroller; density slider hides in favor of a fixed two-column masonry; cart drawer becomes a swipe-up sheet with a sticky 'View cart' tab.
